mechanism_narrative: A lower FEDFUNDS level signals easier discounting and less policy drag on equity valuations, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is in a declining or already-low state.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not higher than unconditional baseline when FEDFUNDS is falling or below 5%, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration paired with a higher FEDFUNDS rate implies tighter real financial conditions and a greater chance of multiple compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above 3.5% and FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not produce negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of cases, or if CPI inflation spikes without weaker SPX performance for two consecutive rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income growth and softer demand, which should pressure corporate earnings expectations and reduce S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 60-day returns more than half the time, or if the signal loses sign consistency across a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A broad improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS reflects stronger payroll income and retail demand, which lifts near-term earnings momentum and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS and RSAFS improvements fail to outperform baseline on 20-day returns, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A strong INDPRO trend indicates healthy industrial activity and operating leverage, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when production is expanding on a sustained basis.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 3 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if three-month production uptrends do not precede positive 20-day returns more often than not, or if the sign reverses in a rolling 24-month out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp HOUST decline marks a housing slowdown that usually precedes weaker consumer demand and lower cyclical earnings, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days after the downturn becomes persistent.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average for 2 straight months
failure_condition: Reject if sustained housing weakness is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in a majority of instances, or if the signal shows no edge relative to unconditional returns over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth slows while UNRATE rises, the labor market is clearly deteriorating and equity risk pricing should weaken, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth turns negative while UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over the same span
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor-slowdown trigger does not produce below-baseline 20-day returns, or if the effect is replicated by PAYEMS or UNRATE alone without interaction.

mechanism_narrative: In a stable-price environment, a falling FEDFUNDS rate combined with easing CPIAUCSL inflation lowers real rates and boosts equity valuation multiples, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation is below 2.5% and FEDFUNDS is falling over the last 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint disinflation-and-easing condition does not yield positive 5-day returns more often than not, or if performance vanishes once inflation is controlled for.
